skill.name: faceswap
skill.description: Create AI face swaps in short videos using the verging.ai API, local media files, remote image URLs, and supported video URLs.
skill.summary: Create short AI face swap videos through verging.ai with local or remote media inputs.
skill.author: verging.ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, network access, API key handling, temporary file use, and weak-crypto matches. Review confirmed the command, network, credential, and filesystem risks as intended but security-sensitive behavior for a media upload workflow. No prompt injection or malicious intent was found, and the weak cryptography alerts are false positives.

medium_findings.title: External Tool Execution With User-Supplied Media Inputs
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run npx, yt-dlp, curl, ffprobe, and ffmpeg commands using user-provided paths and URLs. This is required for the workflow, but it creates command execution and input handling risk if arguments are not quoted or validated.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files directly document shell commands that process user-controlled video and image inputs. The commands are normal for this skill, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: External API Uploads Include Sensitive Media and Credentials
medium_findings.description: The workflow sends an API key in Authorization headers and uploads video and face image data to verging.ai and a presigned storage URL. This behavior is disclosed and central to the service, but users must understand that personal media leaves the local machine.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key header and multipart upload instructions are explicit. The endpoints appear aligned with the claimed face swap service, which reduces concern about malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Temporary Media Files Stored Under /tmp
medium_findings.description: The instructions place downloaded, trimmed, and uploaded media under /tmp/verging-faceswap. Temporary storage is practical, but face images and videos may remain readable until cleanup is performed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Multiple instructions reference the same temporary directory and media filenames. The skill also tells users to clean up, so the issue is a privacy hygiene concern.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Service URLs Are Expected API Documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged verging.ai, YouTube, and example URL references. These links document the intended external service and example inputs, with no evidence of hidden endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible user-facing documentation for the advertised service. I did not find evidence that they are concealed or unrelated to the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm matches, but the referenced lines describe face swap metadata or a Markdown table. No evidence of cryptographic code was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural language or Markdown formatting, not hash or cipher usage. This makes the weak-crypto finding highly likely to be a scanner false positive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Execution Plus Network Upload Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill combines local media commands with network uploads and credentialed API calls. This combination is expected for the feature but requires careful user consent and argument handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented execution flow explicitly combines downloads, ffmpeg processing, API upload, polling, and result retrieval. It matches the analyzer heuristic but does not show malicious intent.

content.user_title: Create AI Face Swaps from Videos
content.value_statement: Video face swaps often require complex editing tools and manual upload steps. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through trimming, uploading, job creation, polling, and optional download.
actual_capabilities: Accepts local video files or supported video URLs as source input.
Accepts local face images or remote image URLs for the swap face.
Trims videos to the service limit with ffmpeg when needed.
Uploads video media and face images through the verging.ai API workflow.
Polls face swap job status and reports the result URL.
Can download the completed result when requested.
limitations: Requires a valid VERGING_API_KEY or an API key provided by the user.
Requires local command line tools such as yt-dlp, ffmpeg, ffprobe, and curl.
Uploads user media to an external service for processing.
Supports a maximum processed video duration of 30 seconds.

use_cases.title: Prototype Short Social Videos
use_cases.description: Create quick face swap clips for concept testing before using full editing software.

use_cases.title: Automate Repeat Media Tests
use_cases.description: Run the same face swap workflow across local files with consistent trimming and output handling.

use_cases.title: Prepare Demo Assets
use_cases.description: Generate short AI video examples from approved source clips and consented face images.

prompt_templates.title: Swap a Local Clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Use faceswap with ./video.mp4 and ./face.jpg. Keep normal quality and return the result URL.

prompt_templates.title: Trim Before Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use faceswap on ./interview.mp4 with ./headshot.jpg. Process only seconds 5 through 25 and download the result.

prompt_templates.title: Use a Remote Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Use faceswap with this video URL and this face image URL. Verify required tools first, then process in normal mode.

prompt_templates.title: Create an HD Result
prompt_templates.prompt: Use faceswap in HD mode for ./scene.mp4 and ./portrait.jpg. Check credits first, trim to 20 seconds, and save the result to ./outputs.

output_examples.input: Local video and local face image with default settings.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks credits, uploads the trimmed video, creates the face swap job, and returns a completed result link.

output_examples.input: A 60 second source video with a requested 10 to 25 second segment.
output_examples.output: The assistant trims the selected segment, processes only the allowed duration, and reports the final download location.

output_examples.input: Remote video URL, remote face image URL, and HD mode.
output_examples.output: The assistant downloads inputs, confirms the higher credit cost, submits an HD job, and tracks progress until completion.

best_practices: Use only media where you have consent and rights for face replacement.
Set the API key through VERGING_API_KEY instead of pasting it into prompts.
Delete temporary media files after each completed job.
anti_patterns: Do not use private, biometric, or sensitive media without explicit consent.
Do not publish generated videos without reviewing legal and platform requirements.
Do not run the workflow on untrusted URLs without checking the source first.

faq.question: Does this skill work without a verging.ai account?
faq.answer: No. It needs a verging.ai API key to check credits, upload media, and create jobs.

faq.question: Can it process long videos?
faq.answer: It is designed for short clips. The documented maximum processed duration is 30 seconds.

faq.question: Does my media stay local?
faq.answer: No. The workflow uploads video and face image data to verging.ai and related storage endpoints.

faq.question: Which local tools are required?
faq.answer: The documented workflow uses yt-dlp, ffmpeg, ffprobe, and curl.

faq.question: Can I use Claude, Codex, or Claude Code with it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish in a marketplace?
faq.answer: It is publishable with warnings because it uses external commands, credentials, network uploads, and temporary media files.
